steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is specific: indicate how Fulton, Missouri got its name. Any choice that doesn't answer that exact question fails, even if it contains true information from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Fulton Street is in New York City, where the steamboat *Clermont* was designed in 1807.’: This is about Fulton Street in NYC and the steamboat — it says nothing about how Fulton, Missouri got its name. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Fulton, Missouri, is named after Robert Fulton, designer of the first commercially successful steamboat.’: Directly states that Fulton, MO is named after Robert Fulton and provides relevant context about who he was. This accomplishes the goal. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Designed in 1807 in New York City, *Clermont* was the first commercially successful steamboat.’: This is about the steamboat's design — it never mentions Fulton, Missouri or how it got its name. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Fulton, Missouri, shares its name with Fulton Street in New York City.’: This implies the city and the street share a name, but omits the actual source of the name (Robert Fulton). It doesn't explain how the city got its name. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
